title: Annual content re-validation vs. updated EPA/WHO guidance
context: Drinking-water regulations change. The 2024 PFAS MCLs represent a major rule change that required contaminant library updates. The Lead and Copper Rule is under ongoing revision. WHO Guidelines for Drinking-water Quality are updated periodically. Any contaminant entry that references a superseded limit or cites an outdated source is a content accuracy failure. This recurring annual maintenance task re-validates all contaminant library entries against the current EPA and WHO guidance, flags any drift, and triggers expert re-review where needed.
objective: Re-validate all published contaminant library entries against the current EPA regulatory reference (MCL/MCLG tables, Lead and Copper Rule, PFAS NPDWR) and current WHO Guidelines: (1) check each entry's cited limits against current EPA/WHO values; (2) flag any drift between the published entry and the current regulatory value; (3) trigger expert re-review for any entry with a changed limit or updated guidance; (4) update retrieval dates; (5) record findings in the re-validation log with a named reviewer.
acceptanceCriteria: All published contaminant library entries checked against current EPA MCL/MCLG tables and WHO Guidelines
Any entry where a cited limit differs from the current regulatory value is flagged and triggering expert re-review
Expert re-review completed for all flagged entries; entries updated and re-approved by an approver distinct from the author
Retrieval dates updated for all entries checked
Re-validation findings recorded in an append-only re-validation log with reviewer name and date
output: Document: annual content re-validation report with findings, drift flags, expert re-review records, and updated contaminant library entries.
